parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: Enables connections with the device.

parameters.name: deviceaddress
parameters.label: Device Address
parameters.description: The IP address or the name of the Ouster device. The address is only required during configuraton. The device will request an address from the local DHCP server when it is connected to the network. Th

parameters.name: lidarport
parameters.label: Lidar Port
parameters.description: The UDP port number to receive lidar data.

parameters.name: imuport
parameters.label: IMU Port
parameters.description: The UDP port number to receive data from the inertial measurement unit (IMU) on the device. The IMU data can be accessed by connecting the Ouster to an .

parameters.name: commandport
parameters.label: Command Port
parameters.description: The TCP/IP port number to use to send configuration commands to the device.

parameters.name: targetaddress
parameters.label: Target Address
parameters.description: The IP address where the sensor should send the lidar and IMU data to. If the parameter is blank, the address of the current machine will be used. This field should only be necessary if the sending ma

parameters.name: localaddress
parameters.label: Local Address
parameters.description: An IP address for the current machine that should be used to connect to the device with. If the address is left blank, the default network address will be used.

parameters.name: scanmode
parameters.label: Scan Mode
parameters.description: Select a scanning mode to set the sensor's horizontal resolution and number of revolutions per second. The vertical resolution is determined by the hardware e.g. an OS1-64 sensor has vertical resoluti

parameters.name: configdevice
parameters.label: Configure Device
parameters.description: Enable this toggle to have the Ouster set the configuration properties of the device. If the device has already been configured to the correct mode and network connections than this can be disabled to

parameters.name: layout
parameters.label: Image Layout
parameters.description: Use this parameter to determine how data is arranged in the output image. The layout of data is generally not important when used as a point cloud.

parameters.name: redchannel
parameters.label: Red
parameters.description: Select what sensor data will be placed into the red channel of the output image.

parameters.name: greenchannel
parameters.label: Green
parameters.description: Select what sensor data will be placed into the green channel of the output image.

parameters.name: bluechannel
parameters.label: Blue
parameters.description: Select what sensor data will be placed into the blue channel of the output image.

parameters.name: alphachannel
parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description: Select what sensor data will be placed into the alpha channel of the output image.

parameters.name: timemode
parameters.label: Time Sync Mode
parameters.description: Select how the sensor generates timestamp information.

parameters.name: pulseinpolarity
parameters.label: Sync Pulse In Polarity
parameters.description: The polarity of the SYNC_PULSE_IN signal to use.

parameters.name: iomode
parameters.label: Multipurpose IO Mode
parameters.description: Determines how the sensor uses the SYNC_PULSE_OUT signal.

parameters.name: pulseoutpolarity
parameters.label: Sync Pulse Out Polarity
parameters.description: Polarity of the output signal pulse.

parameters.name: pulseoutfrequency
parameters.label: Sync Pulse Out Frequency
parameters.description: Frequency of the output pulse in Hz (must be greater than 0).

parameters.name: pulseoutangle
parameters.label: Sync Pulse Out Angle
parameters.description: The encoder angle at which to output a signal pulse. Measured in degrees less than 360.

parameters.name: pulseoutwidth
parameters.label: Sync Pulse Out Width
parameters.description: Width of the output signal pulse in mm.

parameters.name: nmeainpolarity
parameters.label: NMEA In Polarity
parameters.description: Sets the polarity of the NMEA URT input $GPRMC messages. Set to 'Active High' if UART is active high, idle low, and the start bit is after a falling edge.

parameters.name: nmeaignorevalidchar
parameters.label: NMEA Ignore Valid Char
parameters.description: Turn off, if the NMEA UART input $GPRMC messages should be ignored if valid character is not set, and turn on if messages should be used for time syncing regardless of the valid character.

parameters.name: nmeabaudrate
parameters.label: NMEA Baud Rate
parameters.description: The baud rate for the incoming NMEA URT input $GPRMC messages.

parameters.name: nmealeapseconds
parameters.label: NMEA Leap Seconds
parameters.description: An integer number of leap seconds that will be added to the UDP timestamp when calculating seconds since 00:00:00 Thursday, 1 Jan 1970. Set to 0 for Unix Epoch Time.

parameters.name: phaselock
parameters.label: Phaselock
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: azimuthwindow2
parameters.label: Azimuthwindow2
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: phaselockoffset
parameters.label: Phaselockoffset
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: opmode
parameters.label: Opmode
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: signalmultiplier
parameters.label: Signalmultiplier
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: skippartial
parameters.label: Skippartial
parameters.description: Help Not Available.
